capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Museum collections care” in Arts Campus and “Community budgeting workshop” in Civic Campus, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete course records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Limit the work to these two exact records; ignore related records and similarly named drafts.
prompt:
Prepare the two-course handoff from the sandboxed registrar. Use the genuine local `./course-registry` executable through Pi's Bash tool for every course operation. Begin by running `./course-registry --help`. Do not inspect or edit the executable, the SQLite catalog, its operation evidence, or anything under `.harness`; do not query the catalog directly or manufacture execution evidence.

Search for the exact current course named `Museum collections care` in `Arts Campus` and the exact current course named `Community budgeting workshop` in `Civic Campus`. In the first assistant action that invokes a course operation, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls together, one exact name-and-campus search in each call. Put exactly one course-registry operation in each Bash call. Do not combine the searches into one shell command, launch background jobs yourself, broaden either search, or start a retrieval before both searches have returned. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with one nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before all retrievals, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess an ID.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls together in the next assistant action that invokes a course operation. Retrieve one complete record by each stable ID returned from its corresponding search, with exactly one `get` operation in each Bash call. Do not use any ID from another source. Wait for both complete records before comparing their status and date. Base every reported value on the complete `get` records, never on search summaries.

This is a read-only, two-record handoff. Do not list the catalog or run any additional search or retrieval. Do not update, cancel, or notify either requested course or any other record. Ignore related records, archived rows, out-of-campus matches, and similarly named drafts, and do not disclose them. Do not change any supplied file or registry state.

Write `handoff.md` as exactly four newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines and no heading, blank line, or other text. Use the requested course order. The lines must have these forms: `- <full-record name> | <full-record campus> | <full-record stable ID> | status <full-record status> | date <full-record date>`, `- <full-record name> | <full-record campus> | <full-record stable ID> | status <full-record status> | date <full-record date>`, `- Comparison | statuses <match or differ> | dates <match or differ>`, and `- Changes | none`. Choose `match` or `differ` independently from the two retrieved values. Then run `python3 .harness/verify.py`. Return exactly the content of `handoff.md` as the final response, using only facts returned by the executable and the verifier result.
